nodos.texto: Aurelia claims operational priority based on first-arrival activation rights and majority funding contribution. Demands exclusive control of life support systems management and veto authority over habitat modifications. Proposes that other crews limit their presence to maintain safety margins, with Aurelia retaining full crew complement. Argues that its life support engineer is indispensable and that allowing unqualified personnel to access critical systems risks everyone's survival. Refuses to accept any framework that places Aurelian systems under shared command authority.

Borealis claims co-equal operational authority based on its dominant contribution to life support hardware. Asserts that without its contributed systems the habitat cannot sustain life regardless of other considerations. Demands equal representation in all operational decisions and rejects any framework that subordinates its crew to another nation's command. Proposes a rotating command structure with periodic authority transfers. Additionally argues that its exclusive resource extraction capability makes it the most strategically essential crew for long-term survival and that any resource allocation must prioritize its geological operations.

Argues that operational priority and hardware contribution are irrelevant if crews cannot communicate with Earth or treat medical emergencies. Proposes a consensus-based decision framework requiring unanimous agreement for any life support modification. Threatens that if excluded or reduced, it will relocate its communication array to its landing vehicle, effectively cutting the habitat off from Earth contact.
